title: Will BitTorrent (BTT) Surge? Price Analysis
content: Analyzing the price movement of BitTorrent requires looking at both current market volatility and long-term historical context. As of April 24, 2026, BTT is trading at $0.00000032 with a 24-hour change of -0.03%. Recent historical data shows a 30-day performance decline of 2.95% and a 1-year performance drop of 51.68%. Compared to the broader crypto landscape, where sectors like DN-404 and NFT Launchpads have seen significant activity, BTT’s performance reflects the sensitivity of large-cap legacy tokens to shifts in liquidity. When comparing the protocol to peers such as [Filecoin](/filecoin) or [Axie Infinity](/axie-infinity), the disparity in adoption-driven price action remains notable. Technical support levels are currently tested near the all-time low of approximately $0.00000030, while resistance is heavily anchored by the historical peak of $0.00000343, which occurred on January 21, 2022. For BTT to see a substantial reversal, a confluence of increased network utility and broader macro recovery is required. The TokenRadar Risk Score of 8 underscores the high volatility associated with this asset, where price swings of over 50% on an annual basis have been observed. Objective market analysis suggests that without a clear catalyst or fundamental shift in usage metrics, price trends remain tethered to the general risk-on sentiment of the total crypto market, which is currently navigating a period of fluctuating BTC dominance.
